facts: Georgia's plastic manufacturing sector produces a wide range of plastic products for various industries, from automotive to consumer goods.
The industry increasingly prioritizes sustainability and recycling, responding to global demand for eco-friendly solutions and reducing waste.
Altium Packaging supplies plastic packaging solutions to the automotive industry from its Atlanta, Georgia headquarters.

ES-Indorama Ventures aims to recycle 50 billion plastic bottles annually by 2025 while supplying sustainable chemical manufacturing and recycled PET/polymer solutions for the automotive industry from Duluth, Georgia.
CKS Packaging Inc operates 27 plants across the United States employing over 1,100 individuals, providing blow-molded plastic containers for the automotive sector from Atlanta, Georgia.
Sumitomo (SHI) Demag North America manufactures plastic injection molding machinery for the automotive sector from its Suwanee, Georgia headquarters, with a headcount of 1001-5000.
Silon Us manufactures technical compounds tailored for the automotive sector from Dunwoody, Georgia, employing 11 to 50 people.

Ecoplastic America Corporation is an automotive parts manufacturer and tier-1 supplier for Hyundai Motor Group, producing bumpers, consoles, trims, and plastic molds from Register, Georgia, with a workforce of approximately 501-1000 employees.
Ecoplastic America is actively building a manufacturing team in Bulloch County, Georgia.
Southern Polymer distributes polypropylene, polyethylene, and polystyrene for the automotive industry from Atlanta, Georgia, as part of the Audia Group.
Custom Profiles, Inc. manufactures custom thermoplastic products for the automotive industry from Fitzgerald, Georgia, employing 29 people.
KTX America Corporation is an automotive parts manufacturer producing plastic components for vehicle interiors and exteriors in Toccoa, Georgia, with approximately 91 employees.
KTX America serves as a tier 1 supplier to BMW and Daimler.
KTX America utilizes advanced technologies including multi-component injection molding and high-gloss painting.
Elitech manufactures plastic components and molds for batteries from its Atlanta, Georgia location, employing over 150 people.
Nexus Circular transforms hard-to-recycle plastics into renewable plastic products in Atlanta, Georgia, securing $150 million in funding in January 2023.

Thomson Plastics, Inc. provides plastic injection molding and assembly services for the automotive market from facilities in Thomson, Georgia, Lexington, North Carolina, and Howell, Michigan, employing approximately 173 people.
Thomson Plastics operates machines capable of exerting up to 3700 tons of clamping force.
OECHSLER Motion, Inc. provides plastic injection molding and additive manufacturing for the automotive sector from Acworth, Georgia.
